title: Package marked private — blocks npm publish
impact: npm publish and changeset publish both hard-fail. The package will never reach consumers.
evidence: "private": true is set in package.json
fix_suggestion: Remove "private": true. If scoped access control is needed, use "publishConfig": { "access": "public" }.

title: Package exports and main/module/types fields point at TypeScript source, not compiled dist
impact: Consumers who install the npm package receive raw .ts files that don't exist in the published package (only dist/ is in files array). The package is non-functional post-publish.
evidence: "main": "./src/index.ts", "module": "./src/index.ts", "exports": { ".": { "import": "./src/index.ts" } }. Compiled output exists at dist/.
fix_suggestion: Update all fields to dist/: "main": "./dist/index.js", "module": "./dist/index.js", "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", exports pointing at dist/components/*/index.js and dist/components/*/index.d.ts.

title: lit listed as direct dependency instead of peerDependency — causes dual Lit instances
impact: Every consumer gets their own bundled copy of Lit. Two Lit instances at runtime cause silent failures: custom element registration breaks, reactive properties stop working, Lit's template cache is split.
evidence: "dependencies": { "@helix/tokens": "*", "lit": "^3.3.2" }. vite.config.ts correctly externalizes Lit but package.json must declare the peer relationship.
fix_suggestion: Move lit to peerDependencies: { "lit": "^3.0.0" } and keep in devDependencies for local development.

title: hx-checkbox completely removed from keyboard tab order via tabindex=-1
impact: Keyboard-only users and screen reader users cannot reach or activate the checkbox via Tab navigation. WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 (Level A) failure. Blocks all non-mouse users from completing clinical workflows.
evidence: tabindex="-1" on native <input type="checkbox">. The <label> has no tabindex. The focus() override works programmatically but Tab navigation skips the component entirely.
fix_suggestion: Remove tabindex="-1" from the native input (or change to tabindex="0"). Keep visually-hidden CSS. The existing .checkbox__input:focus-visible ~ .checkbox__box rule will then fire correctly.

title: hx-radio has no keyboard Space/Enter activation handler
impact: Keyboard users cannot select a radio button by pressing Space (required by ARIA APG for role=radio). WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 failure for a critical clinical data entry control.
evidence: Component sets role=radio on host and has no keydown event listener. Only interaction path is a @click handler on the inner .radio div. No e.key === ' ' handling exists anywhere in the file.
fix_suggestion: Add keydown handler: if (e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); this._handleClick(); }

title: @fires JSDoc uses stale wc-after-close prefix — CEM documents non-existent event
impact: CEM (Quality Gate 5) documents wc-after-close as the fired event. Consumers and Drupal behaviors wire up dead listeners that silently never fire.
evidence: Line 23: @fires {CustomEvent} wc-after-close. Line 163: new CustomEvent('hx-after-close', ...).
fix_suggestion: Change annotation to @fires {CustomEvent} hx-after-close. Regenerate CEM with npm run cem.

title: @fires JSDoc uses stale wc-card-click prefix — CEM documents non-existent event
impact: CEM accuracy failure. Consumers listening for wc-card-click receive nothing. Card interaction integrations silently fail.
evidence: Line 20: @fires {CustomEvent} wc-card-click. Line 91: new CustomEvent('hx-card-click', ...).
fix_suggestion: Change to @fires {CustomEvent<{url: string, originalEvent: MouseEvent}>} hx-card-click. Regenerate CEM.

title: @fires JSDoc uses stale wc-submit/wc-invalid/wc-reset prefixes in hx-form — three events wrong
impact: Three events documented with wrong names in CEM. Drupal form submission behaviors and any CEM-reading consumer subscribes to phantom event names. Form submissions silently not handled.
evidence: Lines 24-26: @fires wc-submit, @fires wc-invalid, @fires wc-reset. Implementation dispatches hx-submit (219), hx-invalid (238), hx-reset (252).
fix_suggestion: Update all three @fires annotations to hx-submit, hx-invalid, hx-reset. Regenerate CEM.

title: _text-input.css uses deprecated --wc- prefix throughout — 19 token references break theming
impact: Every --hx- token override a consumer sets has zero effect on these selectors. Unified theming is impossible — Shadow DOM components respond to --hx-* but Light DOM form styles operate on a completely separate namespace.
evidence: border: var(--wc-input-border-color, ...), opacity: var(--wc-opacity-disabled, 0.5), etc. — 19 occurrences of --wc- prefix.
fix_suggestion: Mechanical find-and-replace --wc- to --hx- throughout the file.

title: _lists.css uses deprecated --wc- prefix throughout — 20 token references break prose theming
impact: Consumers setting --hx-prose-color, --hx-prose-font-size, or spacing tokens see no effect on list elements — the most common content type in CMS-rendered prose.
evidence: color: var(--wc-prose-color, var(--wc-color-neutral-700, ...)), font-size: var(--wc-prose-font-size, ...) — 20 occurrences.
fix_suggestion: Replace all --wc- with --hx- throughout the file.

title: _media.css uses deprecated --wc- prefix throughout — 14 token references break media theming
impact: Images, figures, videos, iframes, and embedded media all ignore --hx- token overrides. CKEditor images and Drupal media embeds are completely un-themeable.
evidence: border-radius: var(--wc-border-radius-sm, 0.25rem), margin-bottom: var(--wc-space-6, ...) — 14 occurrences.
fix_suggestion: Replace all --wc- with --hx- throughout the file.

title: Global bare label selector in _field.css leaks styles to entire page
impact: When form.css is imported globally (its intended use in Drupal), a bare label {} rule applies form-specific styles to every label on the page — navigation labels, filter labels, widget labels.
evidence: label { display: flex; align-items: baseline; color: var(--hx-input-label-color, ...); } — no parent scope.
fix_suggestion: Scope to hx-form label {} to mirror the scoped pattern in form.scoped.css.

title: All lifecycle hooks use Map<string, unknown> instead of PropertyValues<this>
impact: Typos in .has() key names are never caught at compile time. System-wide violation of the project's strict TypeScript mandate.
evidence: override updated(changedProperties: Map<string, unknown>): void in hx-text-input.ts, hx-checkbox.ts, hx-select.ts, hx-switch.ts, hx-radio-group.ts, hx-radio.ts, hx-prose.ts, hx-textarea.ts (9 files).
fix_suggestion: import { PropertyValues } from 'lit'; override updated(changedProperties: PropertyValues<this>): void in all 9 files.

title: All test files import non-existent Wc* type aliases — no type safety in tests
impact: Component exports HelixButton/HelixCard/etc. No WcButton/WcCard type alias exists. Tests pass only because Vitest transpiles without type-checking. All property accesses on el in tests are effectively untyped.
evidence: import type { WcButton } from './hx-button.js' — no such export. All 14 test files have this pattern.
fix_suggestion: Add export type WcButton = HelixButton; to each component, or update all test imports: import type { HelixButton as WcButton } from './hx-button.js'.

title: sideEffects glob marks all component source files as non-tree-shakeable
impact: Bundlers refuse to eliminate any component even when only one is imported. Per-component entry points become useless — every consumer bundles all components. The glob references src/ paths not in published dist/, making it both over-inclusive and non-functional post-publish.
evidence: "sideEffects": ["**/*.css", "src/components/**/*.ts"]
fix_suggestion: "sideEffects": false (or ["dist/**/*.css"] if CSS files need side-effect preservation). Remove the component .ts glob entirely.

title: No prepublishOnly hook — quality gates bypassed at publish
impact: A developer can run npm publish without type-check, test, build, or CEM generation. All 7 quality gates are bypassed at the most consequential step in the release pipeline.
evidence: Scripts contain build, type-check, test, cem but no prepublishOnly or prepack.
fix_suggestion: Add "prepublishOnly": "npm run type-check && npm run test && npm run build && npm run cem".

title: No automated npm publish workflow in CI/CD
impact: Publishing is entirely manual with no guards, no channel separation, and no post-publish steps. CLAUDE.md documents an automated @next channel but no such workflow exists.
evidence: Only ci.yml and ci-matrix.yml exist. Neither contains npm publish, changeset publish, NODE_AUTH_TOKEN, or NPM_TOKEN.
fix_suggestion: Create .github/workflows/release.yml using changesets/action for version PRs and automated publishing with NODE_AUTH_TOKEN from repository secrets.

title: Matrix CI tests Node 18 — below declared engines requirement and EOL
impact: Root package.json declares engines >= 20.0.0. Node 18 reached EOL April 2025. Wastes CI and produces misleading results.
evidence: node-version: [18, 20, 22] vs "node": ">=20.0.0"
fix_suggestion: Remove 18 from the matrix. If Node 18 support is intentional, update engines.

title: Matrix CI concurrency group excludes matrix dimensions — runners self-cancel
impact: All 9 matrix runners share one concurrency group key. With cancel-in-progress: true, only one runner survives per ref, defeating the matrix entirely.
evidence: group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }} with cancel-in-progress: true on a 3×3 matrix.
fix_suggestion: Include matrix dimensions in group key: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.node-version }}.

title: No Turborepo remote caching configured in CI — all runs are cold builds
impact: Every CI run performs a full cold build with no cache reuse. On a 9-runner matrix, all 9 runners rebuild from scratch.
evidence: turbo.json has no remoteCache block. Neither CI workflow sets TURBO_TOKEN or TURBO_TEAM.
fix_suggestion: Add TURBO_TOKEN and TURBO_TEAM environment variables to both workflows from repository secrets.

title: @helix/tokens dependency uses wildcard version range
impact: Any breaking change to @helix/tokens is pulled in at install time with no constraint, causing silent cascading failures in consumer deployments.
evidence: "@helix/tokens": "*"
fix_suggestion: Pin to a proper semver range: "@helix/tokens": "^0.0.1"

title: Package name mismatch — @helix/library in package.json vs @wc-2026/library in documentation
impact: External docs, CDN references, or consumer imports using @wc-2026/library will fail to resolve. Creates brand/identity split that confuses consumers.
evidence: "name": "@helix/library" in package.json; CLAUDE.md documents @wc-2026/library.
fix_suggestion: Align the name across all documentation and configuration. Pick one canonical name.

title: Alert close button is 24x24px — fails WCAG 2.5.5 44px touch target requirement
impact: Touch target too small for users with motor impairments. Direct WCAG 2.5.5 Level AA violation. In healthcare, alerts communicate critical clinical information.
evidence: width: var(--hx-space-6, 1.5rem) = 24px; height: var(--hx-space-6, 1.5rem) = 24px.
fix_suggestion: Expand interactive area to 44x44px via padding or pseudo-element without changing visual size.

title: hx-button sm (32px) and md (40px) sizes fall below 44px touch target
impact: Default and small buttons fail WCAG 2.5.5. The md variant is the default — every primary CTA is 4px below minimum. Healthcare kiosk/bedside tablet interfaces are directly affected.
evidence: .button--sm { min-height: var(--hx-size-8, 2rem); } = 32px. .button--md { min-height: var(--hx-size-10, 2.5rem); } = 40px.
fix_suggestion: Increase md min-height to at least 44px. Use transparent padding to expand sm hit area without changing visual size.

title: All hx-switch track sizes fall below 44x44px touch target
impact: sm (32x18px), md (40x22px), lg (48x26px) — none meet the 44px height requirement. WCAG 2.5.5 failure across all three sizes.
evidence: .switch--sm .switch__track { width: 2rem; height: 1.125rem; }. .switch--md height: 1.375rem. .switch--lg height: 1.625rem.
fix_suggestion: Expand touch area to 44px minimum height using transparent border or padding around the track.

title: hx-radio focus ring CSS rule is dead — input is never focused so ring never appears
impact: The component has zero visible focus indicator — a WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) AA failure. Keyboard users cannot see which radio is focused.
evidence: .radio__input:focus-visible ~ .radio__control { outline: ... } — but hx-radio.ts:119 sets tabindex="-1" on the input, so it never receives :focus-visible.
fix_suggestion: Replace the dead sibling rule with :host(:focus-visible) .radio__control { outline: var(--hx-focus-ring-width, 2px) solid ...; }

title: role=alert and aria-live=assertive applied simultaneously — causes double announcement
impact: role=alert is already implicitly aria-live=assertive. Adding both causes JAWS and other screen readers to announce critical health alerts twice, degrading clinical usability.
evidence: <div part="alert" role=${this._role} aria-live=${this._ariaLive}> — when error/warning: role="alert" aria-live="assertive".
fix_suggestion: Remove the explicit aria-live attribute. role=alert provides complete semantics.

title: Interactive hx-card uses div[role=link] without performing browser navigation — semantic mismatch
impact: Screen readers announce 'Navigate to /url, link' but activating the card only fires a custom event. Middle-click doesn't work. Users with disabilities are deceived about the control's behavior.
evidence: role=${isInteractive ? 'link' : nothing}, aria-label="Navigate to ${this.wcHref}" — but only hx-card-click is dispatched, no window.location navigation.
fix_suggestion: Render a native <a> element wrapping the card when wcHref is set, or perform actual browser navigation in _handleClick.

title: helpText excluded from aria-describedby when error is active in hx-checkbox, hx-switch, hx-select
impact: Screen reader users lose contextual guidance when they need it most. For clinical fields, help text may contain safety-critical formatting requirements.
evidence: this.helpText && !hasError ? this._helpTextId : null — help text excluded in error state in hx-checkbox, hx-switch, hx-select. hx-text-input correctly includes both.
fix_suggestion: this.helpText ? this._helpTextId : null — always include help text in aria-describedby when present.

title: hx-card _handleSlotChange creates new closure on every render — event listener leak
impact: Lit sees new function references each render cycle and rebinds all four slot listeners on every update. Old closures cannot be garbage collected. Memory and DOM mutation overhead accumulates.
evidence: private _handleSlotChange(slotName: string) { return (e: Event) => { ... }; } called in render as @slotchange=${this._handleSlotChange('image')} four times.
fix_suggestion: Replace the factory with four bound class-field arrow methods: private _handleImageSlotChange = (e: Event): void => { ... }

title: hx-card _hasSlotContent is a plain object not @state — reactive updates use manual requestUpdate
impact: Mutating _hasSlotContent[slotName] does not trigger Lit's update pipeline. requestUpdate() compensates but fires unconditionally even when the value hasn't changed.
evidence: private _hasSlotContent: Record<string, boolean> = { image: false, ... }; this.requestUpdate(); // manual
fix_suggestion: @state() private _hasSlotContent: Record<CardSlotName, boolean>. Use spread on assignment. Remove manual requestUpdate() calls.

title: @slot JSDoc references non-existent <wc-radio> — CEM slot documentation is wrong
impact: CEM slot documentation tells consumers to put <wc-radio> inside <hx-radio-group>. <wc-radio> is not a registered element. Consumers following docs get silent rendering failures.
evidence: Line 9: @slot - '<wc-radio>' elements. Implementation at line 162: querySelectorAll('hx-radio').
fix_suggestion: Change all <wc-radio> references to <hx-radio> in JSDoc. Also fix hx-radio.ts:8 which says 'inside <wc-radio-group>'.

title: super.updated() not called in HelixRadio.updated() — breaks Lit lifecycle contract
impact: Omitting super.updated() drops internal Lit bookkeeping and can cause subtle issues with _$changedProperties tracking in extended components.
evidence: override updated(changedProperties: Map<string, unknown>): void { ... } — no super.updated() call.
fix_suggestion: Add super.updated(changedProperties); as the first line of the method.

title: hasError in hx-checkbox ignores _hasErrorSlot — error styling absent for slotted errors
impact: The .checkbox--error CSS class is never applied when error comes via slot, so the error border styling is absent even though aria-describedby correctly references the error slot.
evidence: const hasError = !!this.error; — missing || this._hasErrorSlot. hx-text-input correctly has: const hasError = !!this.error || this._hasErrorSlot;
fix_suggestion: const hasError = !!this.error || this._hasErrorSlot;

title: setFormValue called twice on every keystroke in hx-text-input — in event handler and updated()
impact: Every user keystroke calls _internals.setFormValue() twice. Redundant DOM manipulation on every input event. Same pattern in hx-textarea.
evidence: _handleInput: this.value = target.value; this._internals.setFormValue(this.value). updated(): if (changedProperties.has('value')) { this._internals.setFormValue(this.value); }
fix_suggestion: Remove setFormValue from _handleInput/_handleChange. Let updated() be the single source of truth.

title: hx-textarea auto-resize direct DOM mutation fights Lit render cycle
impact: Setting target.style.height in the input handler runs before Lit's update cycle. Lit re-renders can reset the inline style causing height flickering on every keystroke. Forces synchronous reflow on every keypress, impacting INP.
evidence: this.value = target.value; target.style.height = 'auto'; target.style.height = `${target.scrollHeight}px`;
fix_suggestion: Move auto-resize logic into updated() after changedProperties.has('value'), targeting this._textarea. Wrap in requestAnimationFrame to avoid blocking the input event.

title: preserveModules not set in Rollup config — shared chunks defeat per-component tree-shaking
impact: Without preserveModules: true, Rollup creates shared chunk files. A consumer importing only hx-button pulls in the entire shared chunk containing code from all components.
evidence: chunkFileNames: 'shared/[name]-[hash].js' confirms shared chunks form. No preserveModules: true in rollupOptions.output.
fix_suggestion: Add preserveModules: true, preserveModulesRoot: 'src' to Rollup output options.

title: Duplicate event listener registration risk in hx-radio-group connectedCallback — Drupal AJAX risk
impact: If the element is moved in the DOM via Drupal AJAX, connectedCallback fires again before disconnectedCallback cleans up, registering duplicate listeners. Every radio selection fires hx-change twice, potentially triggering clinical workflows twice.
evidence: this.addEventListener('hx-radio-select', ...) in connectedCallback with matching removeEventListener in disconnectedCallback but no guard against double-registration.
fix_suggestion: Use AbortController pattern: create new controller in connectedCallback (aborting any previous), pass signal to addEventListener, abort in disconnectedCallback.

title: 21 occurrences of setTimeout(r, 50) instead of await el.updateComplete — flaky tests
impact: Hardcoded 50ms timeouts are flaky under CI load. Tests pass locally but produce false negatives on a loaded runner. Violates the zero-flaky-test mandate.
evidence: await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 50)) in hx-button.test.ts (3x), hx-card.test.ts (2x), hx-select.test.ts (5x), hx-badge.test.ts (3x), hx-radio-group.test.ts (2x), hx-checkbox.test.ts, hx-switch.test.ts, hx-text-input.test.ts (2x), hx-textarea.test.ts (2x).
fix_suggestion: Replace with await el.updateComplete. For 'event should not fire' assertions, drain the microtask queue with updateComplete and check the fired flag.

title: Slotted label and error paths untested across all Drupal-facing form components
impact: The label slot and error slot exist specifically for Drupal Form API integration. A bug here silently breaks accessible name computation for every Drupal-rendered form field — the library's primary consumer.
evidence: _handleLabelSlotChange sets aria-labelledby and assigns IDs to slotted labels. No test in hx-text-input.test.ts, hx-textarea.test.ts, or hx-select.test.ts exercises slot="label" or slot="error".
fix_suggestion: Add tests: slotted label sets aria-labelledby on input; slotted error sets aria-invalid=true and includes error id in aria-describedby.

title: hx-textarea tooLong validity branch completely untested
impact: Clinical notes with maxlength could silently pass validation when over the limit, allowing a form to submit 10,000 characters where 500 were expected, causing database truncation or data loss in patient records.
evidence: Lines 221-225: tooLong: true validity branch. No test in hx-textarea.test.ts exercises value.length > maxlength.
fix_suggestion: Add tests verifying el.validity.tooLong === true and checkValidity() === false when value exceeds maxlength.

title: hx-button keyboard tests call btn.click() after keyboard event — prove nothing about keyboard handling
impact: oneEvent() resolves from btn.click(), not from keyboard handling. If keyboard handler is removed entirely, tests still pass. False confidence about WCAG 2.1.1 compliance.
evidence: btn.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown', { key: 'Enter' })); btn.click(); const event = await eventPromise; — the click fires the event, not the keyboard dispatch.
fix_suggestion: Remove the btn.click() calls. Dispatch the keyboard event alone and await the result.

title: Definite assignment assertions (!) on @query-decorated fields — violates non-null policy
impact: @query decorator returns null before firstUpdated. The ! suppresses this null check. In _updateValidity(), these fields are passed directly to setValidity() — if called before firstUpdated, the browser throws.
evidence: @query('.field__input') private _input!: HTMLInputElement; in hx-text-input.ts, hx-checkbox.ts, hx-select.ts, hx-radio-group.ts, hx-switch.ts, hx-textarea.ts.
fix_suggestion: Declare as HTMLInputElement | null = null. Add if (!this._input) return; guards in methods that access the field.

title: CustomEvent dispatched without explicit generic type parameter — detail is unknown
impact: new CustomEvent('hx-click', { detail: ... }) is inferred as CustomEvent<unknown>. Type-safe event consumption impossible without re-casting at every call site. Affects 17 dispatch sites across all components.
evidence: this.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('hx-click', { bubbles: true, composed: true, detail: { originalEvent: e } })) — no <HxClickDetail> generic.
fix_suggestion: Declare event detail interfaces and provide the generic: new CustomEvent<HxClickDetail>('hx-click', { detail: { originalEvent: e } })

title: Primary color fallback #2563eb (blue) doesn't match library primary #007878 (teal)
impact: If the token stylesheet fails to load, all interactive elements render in the wrong brand color. Focus rings, buttons, checkboxes, badges, radios, and switches are affected.
evidence: background-color: var(--hx-button-bg, var(--hx-color-primary-500, #2563eb)). Design system specifies --hx-color-primary-500: #007878. Same wrong fallback in 14+ locations.
fix_suggestion: Update all --hx-color-primary-500 fallback hex values from #2563eb to #007878 across all component style files.

title: Source maps included in published package — exposes TypeScript component source
impact: .js.map files in dist/ expose full TypeScript component source to any end user opening browser devtools. May be unintentional for an enterprise library.
evidence: sourcemap: true in vite.config.ts. Confirmed .js.map files in dist/shared/ and dist/components/*/
fix_suggestion: sourcemap: false or sourcemap: 'hidden', or add "!dist/**/*.map" to the files array in package.json.

title: Coverage thresholds not enforced — 80% gate is aspirational only
impact: npm run test exits 0 regardless of coverage level. Coverage can silently drop to 0% and CI stays green. The 80%+ coverage Quality Gate 2 has no automated enforcement.
evidence: coverage: { provider: 'v8', enabled: true, reporter: ['text', 'json-summary'] } — no thresholds key.
fix_suggestion: Add thresholds: { lines: 80, functions: 80, branches: 70, statements: 80 } to the coverage config.

title: No prefers-reduced-motion support in any component except hx-badge
impact: All components with transitions apply motion unconditionally. Healthcare applications serve users with vestibular disorders for whom unexpected motion causes physical symptoms.
evidence: transition: background-color var(--hx-transition-fast, 150ms ease), ... in hx-button.styles.ts with no reduced-motion guard. hx-card also has transform: translateY() on hover. hx-badge is the only component with a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block.
fix_suggestion: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .button { transition: none; } } to each component with transitions. For hx-card, also suppress transform.

title: hx-badge dot/pulse indicator has no accessible label for screen readers
impact: When used as a pulsing dot (no slot content), the badge renders an empty animated element. In healthcare dashboards, a pulsing dot typically signals a critical notification — screen reader users receive no information.
evidence: isDot = !this._hasSlotContent && this.pulse renders an empty <span> with no aria-label, role=status, or aria-label.
fix_suggestion: Add ariaLabel property. For dot/pulse variants, document that aria-label MUST be provided by the consumer. Add development warning when missing.

title: hx-textarea character counter not associated with textarea or announced to screen readers
impact: Screen readers cannot discover character limit or current count. For clinical notes with character limits, this is safety-relevant.
evidence:
${display}
— no id, no aria-live, not in aria-describedby.
fix_suggestion: Add id={this._counterId} and aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true" to the counter div. Include _counterId in aria-describedby when showCount is true.

title: hx-checkbox focus ring CSS rule may be dead due to tabindex=-1 on input
impact: The visually-hidden input has tabindex=-1, so :focus-visible on it may never fire via Tab navigation, making the focus ring invisible to keyboard users.
evidence: .checkbox__input:focus-visible ~ .checkbox__box { outline: ... } — hx-checkbox.ts:282: tabindex="-1".
fix_suggestion: Fixed by the CRITICAL tabindex=-1 fix — changing input to tabindex=0 fixes both issues.

title: wc-radio- ID prefix in hx-radio — convention violation visible in DOM
impact: Generates IDs like wc-radio-abc1234 — the only component using the legacy prefix. Visible in the DOM, may break CSS or test selectors relying on the hx- pattern.
evidence: private _inputId = `wc-radio-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 9)}`;
fix_suggestion: private _inputId = `hx-radio-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 9)}`;

title: wcHref property name uses legacy wc- prefix while attribute is hx-href — public API inconsistency
impact: JS property API is card.wcHref but HTML attribute is hx-href. Must be fixed before 1.0 as renaming is a breaking change.
evidence: @property({ type: String, attribute: 'hx-href' }) wcHref = '';
fix_suggestion: Rename property to hxHref. Update all internal usages.

title: hx-select slot template uses inline style display:none — violates no-inline-styles rule
impact: The inline style cannot be overridden via CSS parts or custom properties. Violates the project's engineering standard.
evidence: <slot @slotchange=${this._handleSlotChange} style="display: none;"></slot>
fix_suggestion: Move to hx-select.styles.ts with a class: .field__options-slot { display: none; }

title: hx-select _syncOptions uses shadowRoot.querySelector instead of @query decorator
impact: Live DOM query forces layout recalculation. Called on every slot change and indirectly on every value change. The @query decorator exists to prevent this pattern.
evidence: const slot = this.shadowRoot?.querySelector<HTMLSlotElement>('slot:not([name])')
fix_suggestion: @query('slot:not([name])') private _defaultSlot!: HTMLSlotElement; and use this._defaultSlot.

title: Enter key activates hx-switch — non-standard per ARIA switch specification
impact: ARIA switch role spec states only Space activates a switch. Enter is expected to submit a form. Deviates from APG and may confuse users expecting Enter to submit.
evidence: if (e.key === ' ' || e.key === 'Enter') { e.preventDefault(); this._toggle(); }
fix_suggestion: Remove 'Enter' — respond only to ' ' (Space).

title: Math.random() IDs are not SSR-safe and not deterministic
impact: Server and client generate different IDs, causing hydration mismatches in Next.js/Astro apps. Same pattern in 5 other component files.
evidence: private _inputId = `hx-text-input-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 9)}`;
fix_suggestion: Use a module-level monotonic counter: let _idCounter = 0; private _inputId = `hx-text-input-${++_idCounter}`;

title: Slot-tracking fields not @state in hx-text-input and hx-textarea — manual requestUpdate compensates
impact: Unconditional requestUpdate() fires re-renders even when the value didn't change. Inconsistent with hx-select which correctly uses @state for the same pattern.
evidence: private _hasLabelSlot = false; // no @state. this.requestUpdate(); // manual. Same in hx-textarea.ts:156-157.
fix_suggestion: @state() private _hasLabelSlot = false; Remove manual requestUpdate() calls.

title: Double opacity on disabled hx-button — renders at 25% opacity instead of intended 50%
impact: Both :host([disabled]) and .button[disabled] set opacity: 0.5. Effective opacity is 0.5 x 0.5 = 0.25, far more washed out than the design token intends.
evidence: :host([disabled]) { opacity: var(--hx-opacity-disabled, 0.5); } and .button[disabled] { opacity: var(--hx-opacity-disabled, 0.5); }
fix_suggestion: Remove opacity from .button[disabled]. Keep it only on :host([disabled]).

title: Three test files import non-existent _shadowQueryAll export — dead import
impact: Export is shadowQueryAll (no underscore). Import _shadowQueryAll is dead code. Will cause TypeScript errors when type-check is extended to test files.
evidence: import { ..., _shadowQueryAll, ... } from '../../test-utils.js' in hx-card.test.ts, hx-alert.test.ts, hx-radio-group.test.ts — _shadowQueryAll never called.
fix_suggestion: Remove _shadowQueryAll from import destructuring in all three files.

title: hx-text-input tel, url, and search input types have no tests
impact: 3 of 7 valid input types untested. tel is commonly used for patient contact numbers. Silently broken type attributes cause incorrect mobile keyboard modes.
evidence: Tests cover email, password, number. No test for tel, url, search.
fix_suggestion: Add tests asserting the correct type attribute is set on the inner input for each missing type.

title: axe.run targets el.shadowRoot not host element — misses slotted content accessibility violations
impact: Accessibility violations in slotted content (missing alt text, incorrect heading hierarchy in card slots) are not caught. The zero axe violations gate is incomplete.
evidence: const context = el.shadowRoot ?? el; const results = await axe.default.run(context as unknown as Node, ...)
fix_suggestion: Pass the host element directly: await axe.default.run(el, { ... }). axe-core supports shadow DOM traversal when given the host element.

title: noImplicitReturns and noFallthroughCasesInSwitch absent from base tsconfig
impact: Functions can omit return statements without compile error. Switch cases can silently fall through. Standard components of strict TypeScript discipline are missing.
evidence: tsconfig.base.json has strict: true and noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true but neither noImplicitReturns nor noFallthroughCasesInSwitch.
fix_suggestion: Add both flags to tsconfig.base.json compilerOptions.

title: Record<string, boolean> indexed access produces boolean|undefined under noUncheckedIndexedAccess
impact: this._hasSlotContent['image'] returns boolean|undefined. Used in ?hidden=${!this._hasSlotContent['image']} — undefined coerced to false silently.
evidence: private _hasSlotContent: Record<string, boolean> — a string-indexed record, not a literal-keyed one.
fix_suggestion: Use a discriminated slot name type: type CardSlotName = 'image' | 'heading' | 'footer' | 'actions'; private _hasSlotContent: Record<CardSlotName, boolean>

title: Badge pulse animation duration and easing hardcoded — not tokenized
impact: Teams cannot adjust pulse speed for different patient-facing screens. Healthcare applications need fine-grained control over notification urgency signals.
evidence: animation: wc-badge-pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite — duration and easing are literals.
fix_suggestion: animation: hx-badge-pulse var(--hx-badge-pulse-duration, 2s) var(--hx-badge-pulse-easing, ease-in-out) infinite

title: Container narrow variant uses hardcoded 48rem without intermediate token
impact: The narrow variant cannot be overridden independently. Overriding --hx-container-max-width affects all width variants simultaneously.
evidence: .container__inner--narrow { max-width: var(--hx-container-max-width, 48rem); } — no --hx-container-narrow token.
fix_suggestion: max-width: var(--hx-container-max-width, var(--hx-container-narrow, 48rem));

title: Radio error state skips component token tier — uses raw semantic token directly
impact: Consumer setting --hx-radio-error-color sees it applied in hx-radio Shadow DOM but not in native input[type=radio] inside hx-form. Error state looks different between the two rendering paths.
evidence: border-color: var(--hx-color-error-500, #dc3545) — missing --hx-radio-error-color tier. Checkbox correctly uses var(--hx-checkbox-error-color, var(--hx-color-error-500, ...)).
fix_suggestion: border-color: var(--hx-radio-error-color, var(--hx-color-error-500, #dc3545));

title: hx-prose sets this.style.display=block in connectedCallback — inline style bypass
impact: Inline style overrides consumer CSS setting display on the host. Violates no-inline-styles constraint.
evidence: override connectedCallback(): void { super.connectedCallback(); this.style.display = 'block'; }
fix_suggestion: Remove. Ensure prose.scoped.css contains hx-prose { display: block; }

title: hx-size argType in 4 story files doesn't declare the actual HTML attribute name
impact: Controls panel shows 'size' as the attribute but developers must write hx-size="md" in HTML. Developers copying from the args table write size="md" which is silently ignored.
evidence: argType key: "size" but @property({ attribute: 'hx-size' }) in hx-button.ts, hx-badge.ts, hx-select.ts, hx-switch.ts.
fix_suggestion: Add name: 'hx-size' to the size argType definition in hx-button.stories.ts, hx-badge.stories.ts, hx-select.stories.ts, hx-switch.stories.ts.

title: No active changesets — version history untracked at v0.0.1
impact: No semver record of any changes. Consumers cannot pin to known-good versions or track breaking changes.
evidence: ls .changeset/: only README.md and config.json. package.json version: 0.0.1 — never bumped.
fix_suggestion: Establish the practice: every PR touching src/components/ requires a changeset. Add a CI check enforcing this.

title: wc-badge-pulse keyframe name uses legacy prefix
impact: Inconsistent with hx- prefix convention. Global keyframe name collisions possible in Drupal environments with multiple library versions.
evidence: @keyframes wc-badge-pulse { ... } animation: wc-badge-pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite;
fix_suggestion: Rename to @keyframes hx-badge-pulse and update the animation: reference.

title: CEM analyzer CLI flags conflict with custom-elements-manifest.config.mjs — test files may be processed
impact: The cem script passes inline CLI flags that partially conflict with the config file. --exclude **/*.test.ts is in the config but not in CLI flags, so test files may be analyzed.
evidence: "cem": "custom-elements-manifest analyze --litelement --globs ... --exclude *.stories.ts --exclude *.styles.ts" — missing --exclude **/*.test.ts
fix_suggestion: Either remove inline CLI flags and rely solely on the config file, or add --exclude "**/*.test.ts" to the CLI invocation.

title: hx-form JSDoc class description and summary reference wc-* component names
impact: CEM autodocs and Storybook autodocs render stale wc-* references, confusing consumers about actual tag names.
evidence: Lines 7-10: 'wc-* components'; line 18: 'wc-* form elements'. Implementation correctly uses hx-* at line 163.
fix_suggestion: Replace all wc-* references with hx-* in JSDoc text.

title: hx-form.stories.ts references --wc-* variables in consumer-facing documentation comment
impact: Developers integrating via Storybook see the wrong token prefix in code comments and will set --wc-* tokens that have no effect.
evidence: * happens via the design token layer (`--wc-*` variables).
fix_suggestion: Update to --hx-* variables.

title: hx-switch hx-change event detail missing value property — inconsistent with hx-checkbox
impact: hx-checkbox fires hx-change with { checked, value }. hx-switch fires { checked } only. Consumers using both interchangeably must handle different event shapes.
evidence: detail: { checked: this.checked } in hx-switch vs detail: { checked: this.checked, value: this.value } in hx-checkbox.
fix_suggestion: Add value to hx-switch hx-change event detail. Update @fires JSDoc.

title: aria-disabled redundant on native button element in hx-button
impact: Native ?disabled already provides complete accessibility semantics for <button>. aria-disabled can cause some screen readers to double-announce 'dimmed'.
evidence: ?disabled=${this.disabled} aria-disabled=${this.disabled ? 'true' : nothing}
fix_suggestion: Remove the aria-disabled binding from the inner <button>.

title: hx-form renders form element with no accessible name when used as page landmark
impact: Screen readers (particularly JAWS) announce the form landmark as just 'form' with no descriptor. Unhelpful in multi-form healthcare admin pages.
evidence: <form action=... method=... ?novalidate=...> — no aria-label.
fix_suggestion: Expose a label property on hx-form: <form aria-label=${ifDefined(this.label || undefined)}>

title: AlertVariant type not exported — prevents external type-safe consumption
impact: Consumers who want to type function getAlertVariant(): AlertVariant must duplicate the literal union or use HelixAlert['variant'] indirectly.
evidence: type AlertVariant = 'info' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'error'; — not exported.
fix_suggestion: export type AlertVariant = ... Re-export from hx-alert/index.ts.

title: Redundant optional chaining on Element.prototype.closest
impact: closest is always present on Element. The ?. on closest is misleading noise suggesting closest might be undefined, which it cannot be.
evidence: (e.target as Element)?.closest?.('hx-radio') || radio.checked
fix_suggestion: (e.target as Element)?.closest('hx-radio') || radio.checked

title: Empty .card--default CSS rule occupies space without declarations
impact: Minor — empty rule is parsed by CSS engine and occupies space in the serialized stylesheet string shared across all hx-card instances.
evidence: .card--default { /* Default styling — uses base styles */ } — no declarations.
fix_suggestion: Delete the empty rule.

title: badge--warning dark text color rationale undocumented — silent contrast decision
impact: A future developer may normalize warning badge text to neutral-0 (white) without understanding warning-500 (#eab308) fails 4.5:1 contrast against white, silently breaking WCAG compliance.
evidence: --hx-badge-color: var(--hx-color-neutral-900, #1a1a1a); — no comment explaining contrast rationale while all other variants use neutral-0.
fix_suggestion: Add inline comment: /* Dark text required: warning-500 (#eab308) fails 4.5:1 contrast against #ffffff */

title: Storybook sidebar sort order omits 10 of 13 components — no intentional information architecture
impact: All components except Button, Card, and Text Input appear in alphabetical fallback order, with no logical grouping for discoverability.
evidence: order: [..., 'Components', ['Button', 'Card', 'Text Input', '*'], ...]
fix_suggestion: Expand the Components array to list all components in logical order: primitives first, form controls, then layout.
